rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Use explicit type declarations in AGREE equations to match AADL data types.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Generate range assumptions using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Write output guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid identifiers in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and component implementation binding details
